steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The data shows that full-price restaurant customers leave worse comment ratios than discounted customers. The question asks which principle best explains why higher price (no discount) leads to more negative comments.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: states that customers who dine at full price are more likely to be first-time visitors with no loyalty to the restaurant — this introduces information about customer type that is not supported by the passage or data. ✗
  • Choice B — people who pay more for an experience have higher expectations for it: Directly explains the pattern: paying full price raises the bar, so the same experience is more likely to disappoint, producing worse comment ratios. ✓
  • Choice C: states that customers who receive discounts are more likely to leave comments than those who pay full price — this only re-describes what the data shows about comment frequency without explaining why full-price customers leave worse ratios. ✗
  • Choice D: states that restaurants should lower prices to improve customer satisfaction — this prescribes a pricing policy that goes beyond the data and does not explain the comment-ratio gap. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
